methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
TS Conductor is a Series B climate and energy company founded by Jason Huang that has raised $60M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 9 comparable companies—1 public and 8 private—operating in the same sector and stage. Key private peers include Commonwealth Fusion Systems, Pano AI, Sage Geosystems, Reverion, and CarbonCapture Inc., with valuations ranging from $120M to $5.0B.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $60M total raised as a proxy base and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement, implies a valuation of $114M. Peer-median analysis across the 6-company same-sector, same-stage cohort yields a median valuation of $355M, with a range of $120M to $5.0B. The wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity in technology maturity and capital intensity within frontier energy.

Triangulation of both methods produces a valuation range of $97M (low) to $408M (high), with a median of $234M. The gap between stage-progression and peer-median reflects uncertainty inherent in early-stage frontier-tech comparables. Key sensitivities include technology differentiation relative to the peer set, path to revenue, and capital efficiency. The peer range width suggests that final valuation will hinge on TS Conductor's specific competitive position and execution risk profile within the energy transition landscape.
